title: Best Place To Visit In Ahmedabad
meta_title: Top 10 Best Places To Visit in Ahmedabad - Hotel Cosmopolitan
meta_description: Searching for the most famous tourist places in and around Ahmedabad? Explore the top 10 places to visit with your loved ones in Ahmedabad. View the location, photos, distance, and other information
content:
The river runs through the city, cutting it into halves. In 1997 the state government launched a project with the objective of sustainable development. Whoever visits the town should definitely come to the riverfront to experience the amalgamation of bustling lifestyle and calmness of the river.The project includes parks, sports facilities, and cultural spaces along the river, fostering community engagement. Visitors enjoy the scenic river views, cycle tracks, and events held at the Riverfront.

The statue of unity was built in homage to Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel. It is the tallest monument in the world. This monument is divided into five zones, of which only three are accessible to common crowds. These three zones include a memorial garden, a museum and a view of Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel dam. This 182 m high statue is one of the most recent integrations that was surprisingly built in just 46 months.

Sabarmati Ashram, located on the banks of the Sabarmati River in Ahmedabad, India, is a significant historical site associated with Mahatma Gandhi. Established in 1917, it served as Gandhi's residence and the center for the Indian independence movement. The ashram houses a museum depicting Gandhi's life and philosophy, displaying his personal belongings and letters. Visitors can explore the Hriday Kunj, Gandhi's living quarters, and the Vinoba-Mira Kutir.

This 900-year-old stepwell is situated on the banks of River Saraswati. Although this structure seems similar to other ancient stepwells in India, this one particularly became famous because of its elaborate designs and carved structures on the walls.After centuries submerged, it was excavated in the 1980s, revealing a subterranean masterpiece that attracts visitors with its historical and artistic significance.

The Adalaj Stepwell, located near Ahmedabad, India, is a magnificent five-story stepwell intricately carved in Indo-Islamic architectural style. Built in 1498, it served as a source of water, resting place, and community space. The stepwell displays exquisite carvings of deities, foliage, and mythological scenes. Today, the Adalaj Stepwell stands as a testament to India's rich heritage, drawing visitors and admirers from around the world.

Kankaria Lake, situated in Ahmedabad, India, is a historic artificial lake dating back to the 15th century. Renovated in 2008, it has become a popular recreational spot with an array of attractions. The lakefront offers a scenic promenade, pedal boats, and a zoo. "Balvatika," a children's park, and "Joyrides" amusement park provide entertainment. The lake island houses the Nagina Wadi garden and a summer palace. The lakefront is illuminated at night, creating a vibrant atmosphere.

Constructed in the early 19th century by followers of Bhagwan Swaminarayan, this architectural marvel is a testament to the city's spiritual fervor. The temple's intricate carvings, depicting scenes from Hindu mythology, mesmerize visitors, while its sanctum radiates a sense of divine grace.

Devotees flock to the Swaminarayan Temple to seek solace and offer prayers for prosperity and well-being. Intricately carved marble facades and ornate domes adorn the temple, reflecting the community's reverence for beauty and craftsmanship.

title: Lakes In Ahmedabad
meta_title: Top 5 Lakes in and Around in Ahmedabad - Hotel Cosmopolitan
meta_description: Find the top 5 famous lakes in and around Ahmedabad to spend some relaxing time with your loved ones. Many tourists are unfamiliar with the best gateways in and around Ahmedabad. Check locations and distances
content:
The center of action in this energetic city is this 500-year-old artificial lake. The focal point of the city is Kankaria Lake, which covers 76 acres. Residents who are concerned about their health come to this leisure center in the morning and evening to take a leisurely stroll around the calm surroundings. Not only is there water, but you can also enjoy the companionship of many animal and bird species at the Kankaria Zoo and Kamla Nehru Zoological Garden located on the lakefront. There's a little amusement park with a toy train, roller coaster, and other entertaining things for youngsters. An island in the lake called Nagina Wadi will provide you with memories that are picture-perfect. In addition to food vendors for your culinary delight, you can take pleasure in boat and hot air balloon flights.

One of the most picturesque locations for visitors and city people is Vastrapur Lake, which was renamed Bhakt Kavi Narsinh Mehta Sarovar in 2013. This lake, although being in the center of the city, offers a wonderful retreat from the activity of the metropolis. Health enthusiasts come here in the morning to stretch their muscles because of the lush green flora, circular walk, calm water, and openness. Narmada River water keeps it full all year, even during the hottest summer months. You have a gorgeous green lawn and an outdoor cinema where you can unwind and enjoy time with your family. Savor boating and experience the calm lake's depths.

Located on Dani Limda Road, this man-made lake that dates back to the Mughal era is a must-see lake in Ahmedabad. Having a close relationship to Gandhi Ji's Salt Satyagraha, Chandola Lake is a migratory bird sanctuary. This circular 1200 acre water reservoir is home to painted storks, spoonbills, and cormorants. Grab your binoculars and head to the lakeshore early in the morning to see the gorgeous birds if you'd like to engage in some natural meditation. If you are drawn to historical architecture, you will be captivated by the Shah-e-Alam tomb's arch-dome construction, holy tank, and tastefully constructed entrances.

This over a century-old man-made lake, which is rustic and immaculate, offers the ideal natural escape from the bustling city life. Situated close to Thol village in Gujarat's Mehsana district, this freshwater lake encircled by marshes draws flamingos and Sarus cranes during the winter months. In 1988, the valuable ecosystem was designated as a sanctuary and given the name "Thol Bird Sanctuary." With over 150 species to be found in a limited space, it is a bird watcher's dream come true.

Nal Sarovar is the ideal destination if you're searching for a weekend getaway in the great outdoors with your family. Near Sanand Village, over 210 different species of birds, including flamingos, blackbucks, pink pelicans, and several others, may be seen just 64 kilometers (about an hour's drive) from Ahmedabad's city center. This lake, which was designated as a bird sanctuary in 1969, is a true paradise for birdwatchers and an ideal breeding place for threatened bird species. The body of water contains thirty-six small islands, one of which being Drabla Island, which is reachable by boat. It's the ideal place to spend the weekend because of the watchtower and the regional food. You must come here in the winter if you are an ornithologist, however there is a fee of INR 200.

It is a popular location for foodies and shopaholics and is considered the heart of Ahmedabad. The street cuisine, which comes in a wide range of flavors, is the main attraction of this busy place. It's hardly necessary to introduce Om Sai Parantha; he's one of Ahmedabad's renowned street food lords. There's no denying that jalebi is a popular dish among foodies; if it's also your favorite, you should visit Oswal's in Ahmedabad. You've probably had jalebi with rabdi or curd, but at Oswal's, it comes with papaya, coriander chutney, and fafda. Fafda is a well-known Gujrati snack that is served with a sweet and salty chutney and is made of gram flour.
